title: ficus cyathistipula subsp. pringsheimiana
intro: Ficus cyathistipula subsp. pringsheimiana, also known as the Ficus pringsheimiana, is a species of fig tree native to tropical regions, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, derived from its leaves, bark, and roots. The health benefits of Ficus cyathistipula subsp. pringsheimiana include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various skin conditions, such as wounds, eczema, and acne. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health issues.
